label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking if following these instructions will bring you back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 8 steps backward, then 2 steps backward, then 10 steps forward. The options are Yes or No. First, I need to understand the direction of each movement. The key here is that you're always facing forward. So when you take steps backward, you're moving away from your starting direction, and when you take steps forward, you're moving in the direction you're facing. Let's break it down step by step. Let's assume the starting point is position 0 on a number line. Facing forward means moving in the positive direction. 1. Take 8 steps backward: Since you're facing forward, moving backward would be in the negative direction. So from position 0, moving 8 steps back would take you to position -8. 2. Take 2 steps backward: Still facing forward, moving another 2 steps back. From -8, moving 2 more steps back (negative direction) would take you to -10. 3. Take 10 steps forward: Now, moving forward from -10 in the positive direction. 10 steps forward would take you from -10 to 0. So, after following all three steps, you end up back at position 0, which is the starting point. Therefore, the
